title: Can Poverty Relief Also Reduce Deforestation
subtitle: 减贫与森林保护能否兼得？印尼给出了答案

paragraphs.en: Indonesia has recorded several consecutive years of slower forest loss. One plausible contributor is its conditional cash transfer program for low-income households. Families receive money when children stay in school and basic health requirements are met.
paragraphs.zh: 印度尼西亚已连续数年出现毁林速度放缓。一个可能的促成因素是该国面向低收入家庭的有条件现金转移支付项目。当家庭确保子女持续就学并满足基本健康要求时，便可获得补助。

paragraphs.en: Conventional wisdom often treats poverty reduction and environmental protection as conflicting goals. Yet correlation alone cannot prove that helping poor households must worsen land use outcomes. In rural areas, income buffers can change decisions when weather shocks threaten harvests.
paragraphs.zh: 传统智慧常将减贫与环保视为彼此冲突的目标。但仅凭相关性，并不能证明援助贫困家庭就一定会恶化土地利用结果。在农村地区，当天气冲击威胁收成时，一笔收入缓冲足以改变家庭决策。

paragraphs.en: Satellite evidence from thousands of Indonesian villages linked the program with a sizable reduction in forest clearing. Extra cash may function like informal insurance, reducing pressure to open new land after crop setbacks. The wider lesson is that social policy can produce climate benefits when incentives match local realities.
paragraphs.zh: 来自印尼数千个村庄的卫星证据表明，该项目与毁林面积的大幅减少存在关联。额外的现金或可充当非正式保险，减轻了因作物歉收而被迫开垦新土地的压力。更广泛的启示在于，当激励措施与当地现实相匹配时，社会政策也能带来气候收益。
